wimlib-imagex was not found. Release builds include it under Tools/bin. Otherwise: brew install wimlib
Bootable media for UEFI PCs: ISO, one or more removable drives, erase confirmation.
Data on selected USB drives will be erased
Each drive is formatted as FAT32 (volume label WINSETUP) and receives the Windows installer. Requires install.wim or install.esd and wimlib-imagex (brew install wimlib).
The drive will be formatted and written using the build selected in the app.
FAT32 (WINSETUP) will be applied and Windows setup files will be written.
FAT32 (WINSETUP) will be applied to %lld drives, one after another.
Found %@ on a mounted volume. Build detection without this file is not guaranteed.
Choose an installer image with a sources folder and install.wim or install.esd. For Run all, the ISO is built automatically.
Connect a USB drive and tap Refresh list. Internal disks are hidden.
Writes the selected ISO to the selected drives. The UUP → ISO → USB chain is started with Run all at the bottom.
Choose a Windows build from the UUPDump catalog. The installable .iso file is assembled on the next step — Cache & ISO — after the UUP payload is on disk.
Uses the same steps as Run all: UUP download → ISO conversion → USB write. Select removable drives below, then run the full pipeline.
Connect a USB drive and refresh.
Confirm a build and load download options to see language and edition here.
This build looks like an Insider or preview channel. It may be less stable than retail releases.
Fetching the build list from UUPDump.
The catalog is empty. Tap Refresh catalog to try again.
Nothing matches the current filters or search. Reset filters or broaden your search.
No builds are available to show.
Pick a build in the list, then tap Confirm selection & load options.
Confirm your build and load download options before downloading.
Your list selection doesn’t match the confirmed build. Confirm again or pick Download.
Select a Windows edition in the list above.
The server returned an empty file list. Try another edition or language.
No files could be queued (invalid links). Try again later.
Invalid UUPDump response (missing response field). Check the network and try again.
Network timed out. Try again later.
UUP progress, cache folders, and ISO conversion. Folder: %@
Load Languages & editions on Download ISO so the app can show friendly names here and in the cache list.
These settings apply to new UUP downloads and metadata written into each cache folder. Change them on Download ISO → Parameters (after tapping Languages & editions).
Start a download on Download ISO — progress appears here.
After a UUP download finishes, folders appear here — you can build an ISO from them.
Embedded toolchain found — Homebrew is not required for conversion.

The converter script ships with the app; CLI tools are still required at runtime. Release builds bundle them under Resources/Tools/bin. When building from source, run Scripts/bundle-mac-cli-tools.sh (Homebrew on the dev machine) or install packages manually:
Select at least one USB drive.
Load languages and editions for the selected build first.
Conversion did not produce an ISO path.
Done: image written to the selected drives.
Select a Windows build (for on-drive metadata).
hdiutil: no mount point in plist.
Selected removable drives: %lld. Use the Media step to adjust the list if needed, then Run all.
Choose a build on Source, select USB on Media, then Run all.
USB volume not found after formatting: %@
The ISO has neither sources/install.wim nor sources/install.esd.
On FAT32 a single file cannot exceed ~4 GiB. The image contains files that cannot be split like install.wim:
Not enough space on the USB drive: need about %@, free %@.
[3b/7] Checking file sizes (FAT32 ≤ ~4 GiB per file)…
[3c/7] USB free space vs copy size (without install.wim/esd)…
[4/7] Copying files (no rsync; install.wim/esd skipped — split next)…
Done. You can unplug the drive and use it to install Windows.
